repository.name: AquaVision OmniDrone Knowledge Repository
repository.purpose: Single source of truth for the OmniDrone concept: pitch material, thesis material, engineering specification, research corpus, and chart-ready datasets. Designed to be consumed by a static HTML dashboard via fetch() on these JSON files.
repository.license_note: Internal working document. Cited third-party statistics remain the property of their publishers; see data/07_research/references-bibliography.json for attribution.

READ_THIS_FIRST.provenance_warning: Every quantitative field in this repository carries a `provenance` tag. NEVER cite a figure in a thesis or investor deck without checking that tag first. The tags mean:

provenance_levels.verified_external: Sourced from a named third-party publication (FAO, World Bank, peer-reviewed journal, government agency). A resolvable citation_id exists in references-bibliography.json. Safe to cite with attribution.
provenance_levels.derived_calculation: Computed by AquaVision from one or more `verified_external` inputs. The formula is stated in the record. Safe to cite if you also state the assumptions.
provenance_levels.modeled_projection: AquaVision's forward-looking model (financials, adoption curves, performance targets). This is an ARGUMENT, not a fact. Present as 'we project', never as 'studies show'.
provenance_levels.design_target: An engineering goal the hardware has not yet been measured against. Present as a specification target, not an achieved result.
provenance_levels.illustrative_synthetic: Fabricated sample data whose ONLY purpose is to make the dashboard render and to show data SHAPE. It describes no real pond and no real fish. Must never appear in a thesis results chapter or a pitch as evidence.
provenance_levels.assumption: A stated planning assumption awaiting validation. Listed so reviewers can attack it.

READ_THIS_FIRST.integrity_rule: This repository deliberately does NOT contain fabricated field-trial results, fabricated customer counts, or fabricated revenue. Those sections exist as empty, schema-defined placeholders with `status: awaiting_data`. Fill them only from real trials. A pitch that invents traction is fraud; a pitch that shows a rigorous plan and honest zeros is credible.

consumption_guide.intended_client: Static HTML + vanilla JS or any SPA framework. No build step required.
consumption_guide.load_pattern: Fetch `manifest.json` first, then lazy-load sections from `index.files[]` by `id`.
consumption_guide.id_convention: Every top-level record has a stable `id`. IDs are kebab-case and are treated as permanent primary keys — rename the label, never the id.
consumption_guide.chart_convention: Any object shaped for direct chart rendering lives under data/12_datasets/charts/ and conforms to schema-registry.json#/definitions/chart_payload. It carries `chart_type`, `series[]`, `axes`, `provenance`, and `citation_ids[]`.

sections.label: Meta & Schemas
sections.description: Schema registry, glossary, data dictionary, changelog. Read this to understand every other file.

sections.label: Company & Identity
sections.description: Who AquaVision is: profile, brand system, mission, team structure, milestone roadmap.

sections.label: Problem Space
sections.description: The aquaculture and fisheries problems the OmniDrone addresses, taxonomised, evidenced, and costed.

sections.label: Market & Competition
sections.description: Industry statistics, TAM/SAM/SOM, competitor teardown, customer personas, SWOT and Porter analysis.

sections.label: Solution & ConOps
sections.description: What the OmniDrone does, the value proposition, the use cases, and the concept of operations.

sections.label: Hardware Engineering
sections.description: Airframe, propulsion, reeled probe winch, sensor payload, carriage/ejection system, power budget, dock, BOM.

sections.label: Software, AI & Digital Twin
sections.description: System architecture, data pipeline, ML model cards, digital twin spec, API, telemetry schema, dashboard spec.

sections.label: Research Corpus
sections.description: Literature review, thematic related-studies collections, full bibliography with links, research gaps, theoretical framework, methodology.

sections.label: Operations & Safety
sections.description: Deployment playbook, maintenance, risk register (FMEA), training and certification.

sections.label: Regulatory & Compliance
sections.description: Aviation law, fisheries law, water quality standards, biosecurity, data ethics and privacy.

sections.label: Business Model
sections.description: Business model canvas, pricing, unit economics, financial projections, funding ask, go-to-market.

sections.label: Impact
sections.description: SDG alignment, environmental impact, social and economic impact.

sections.label: Datasets & Charts
sections.description: Chart-ready payloads, KPI definitions, sample telemetry, sample digital twin state.

sections.label: Pitch & Thesis Kit
sections.description: Deck outline, elevator pitches at three lengths, objection handling, thesis chapter outline.

assets.note: Original supplied assets left in repository root. A production webpage should move these to assets/images/ and reference them from brand-identity.json.

highest_priority_gaps: BFAR Philippine Fisheries Profile (operator counts) — blocks market sizing
Primary-source verification of every bibliography entry — zero currently verified
Species-specific water quality thresholds — highest-consequence placeholders
Direct CAAP confirmation of the BVLOS/night-ops pathway and the 7 kg bracket
Real BOM quotes (starting with the sonde) — blocks unit economics
